deck.name: False Friends (Faux-amis)
deck.description: Avoid common mistakes for French speakers by mastering words that look similar but have different meanings.

cards.definition: really / in fact (NOT 'actuellement' which is 'currently')
cards.context: He looks young, but he is actually 45 years old.

cards.definition: in the end / finally (NOT 'éventuellement' which is 'possibly')
cards.context: We waited for hours, but eventually, the bus arrived.

cards.definition: a place where you borrow books (NOT 'librairie' which is 'bookshop')
cards.context: I'm going to the library to study for my exams.

cards.definition: to be present at an event (NOT 'attendre' which is 'to wait')
cards.context: Will you attend the meeting tomorrow afternoon?

cards.definition: to help (NOT 'assister' which is 'to attend')
cards.context: How can I assist you today?

cards.definition: reasonable / practical (NOT 'sensible' which is 'sensitive')
cards.context: It's sensible to wear a warm coat in winter.

cards.definition: easily affected or hurt (French: 'sensible')
cards.context: Be careful what you say; she is very sensitive.

cards.definition: a person in a book or movie (NOT 'caractère' which is 'personality')
cards.context: Who is your favorite character in Harry Potter?

cards.definition: happening now (French: 'actuel')
cards.context: What is your current address?

cards.definition: to trick someone (NOT 'décevoir' which is 'to disappoint')
cards.context: He tried to deceive his parents about where he was going.

cards.definition: to fail to satisfy expectations (French: 'décevoir')
cards.context: I'm sorry to disappoint you, but I can't come tonight.

cards.definition: a danger or risk (NOT 'hasard' which is 'chance')
cards.context: Smoking is a serious health hazard.

cards.definition: something that happens by itself (French: 'hasard')
cards.context: Meeting her there was pure chance.

cards.definition: a diary or a scholarly magazine (NOT 'journal' which is 'newspaper')
cards.context: She writes in her journal every night before bed.

cards.definition: a daily publication of news (French: 'journal')
cards.context: I like to read the newspaper with my morning coffee.

cards.definition: a talk given to a class or audience (NOT 'lecture' which is 'reading')
cards.context: The professor gave an interesting lecture on modern history.

cards.definition: the activity of looking at written words (French: 'lecture')
cards.context: Reading is my favorite way to relax.

cards.definition: a long fictional story (NOT 'nouvelle' which is 'news' or 'short story')
cards.context: He is writing his first novel.

cards.definition: information about recent events (French: 'nouvelles')
cards.context: Have you heard the latest news about the project?

cards.definition: a medical doctor (NOT 'physicien' which is 'physicist')
cards.context: You should consult a physician about your back pain.

cards.definition: a scientist who studies physics (French: 'physicien')
cards.context: Albert Einstein was a famous physicist.

cards.definition: correct or appropriate (NOT 'propre' which is 'clean')
cards.context: Make sure you use the proper equipment for the task.

cards.definition: not dirty (French: 'propre')
cards.context: I need to find a clean shirt for the interview.

cards.definition: a platform for performers or a phase (NOT 'stage' which is 'internship')
cards.context: The band walked onto the stage and began to play.

cards.definition: a temporary job for students or graduates (French: 'stage')
cards.context: He is doing a three-month internship at a law firm.

cards.definition: the act of traveling from one place to another (NOT 'journée' which is 'day')
cards.context: The journey from London to Paris takes about two hours.

cards.definition: a period of 24 hours (French: 'journée')
cards.context: I've had a very long day at work.

cards.definition: to succeed in an exam (NOT 'passer un examen' which is 'to take/sit an exam')
cards.context: I was so relieved when I found out I passed the exam.

cards.definition: to do an exam, whether you succeed or not (French: 'passer un examen')
cards.context: I have to take my final exams next week.

cards.definition: impatient or hard to control (NOT 'rester' which is 'to stay')
cards.context: The crowd grew restive as they waited for the show to start.

cards.definition: cloth or material (NOT 'fabrique' which is 'factory')
cards.context: This dress is made from a very soft fabric.

cards.definition: a building where goods are manufactured (French: 'fabrique' or 'usine')
cards.context: He works in a car factory.

cards.definition: to deliberately not pay attention to someone or something (NOT 'ignorer' which is 'to not know')
cards.context: I tried to say hello, but she completely ignored me.

cards.definition: to lack knowledge about something (French: 'ignorer')
cards.context: I don't know the answer to that question.

cards.definition: to present someone to another person (NOT 'introduire' which is 'to insert')
cards.context: Let me introduce you to my sister.

cards.definition: to place something inside something else (French: 'introduire')
cards.context: Please insert your card into the reader.

cards.definition: specific or special (NOT 'particulier' which can mean 'private' or 'individual')
cards.context: Is there any particular reason why you're late?

cards.definition: belonging to or for the use of one person only (French: 'particulier')
cards.context: This is a private property; please do not enter.

cards.definition: to start something again after a pause (NOT 'résumer' which is 'to summarize')
cards.context: The meeting will resume after a ten-minute break.

cards.definition: to give a brief statement of the main points (French: 'résumer')
cards.context: Can you summarize the main findings of the report?

cards.definition: practical and reasonable (NOT 'sensible' which is 'sensitive')
cards.context: It was a sensible decision to leave early before the traffic started.

cards.definition: easily affected, hurt or offended (French: 'sensible')
cards.context: He is very sensitive about his height.

cards.definition: a group of words or a punishment from a judge (NOT 'sentence' which is more like 'judgment' or 'maxim')
cards.context: Can you write a sentence using this new word?

cards.definition: to provide something that is needed (NOT 'supplier' which is 'to beg')
cards.context: The company supplies office equipment to local businesses.

cards.definition: to ask for something in an anxious or humble way (French: 'supplier')
cards.context: I beg you to reconsider your decision.
